instrumentation.read: This opt-in DSv4 timing hook synchronizes CUDA events inside the standard-EP hot-weight autograd function, so the throughput numbers are diagnostic only.

raw_all_calls_rankmax_sum.read: Raw all-call SDMA timing includes a one-time first-use setup/JIT/handle tax: w2.forward.all_reduce_hot_weight call 1 is about 4.93s on every rank.

approx_step2_call_id_gt288_rankmax_sum.read: In the approximate step-2 window, SDMA saves about 8.6 ms rank-max in backward hot-dW all-reduce but adds about 4.9 ms in forward hot-weight all-reduce, so the net hot-weight microstage improvement is only about 3 ms for the profiled step.

largest_sdma_outliers.read: early backward w3 tail; not present in the late steady window
largest approximate step-2 hot dW call

read.materialized_e2e_context: The six-step no-profile SDMA hot-weight run remains the current retained 12-layer candidate at 8,029.0 tok/GPU/s and 406.522 TFLOPs/GPU, about +0.67% over the previous top-8 standard-EP torch-transport run.
read.timing_conclusion: The timing profile explains why the e2e gain is small. After ignoring one-time SDMA setup, SDMA is not a major transport win for standard-EP hot weights. In the approximate step-2 window, it modestly improves backward hot-dW reduction but slightly worsens forward hot-weight broadcast, for only about 3 ms net rank-max improvement on this microstage.
read.optimization_ladder_decision: Keep the SDMA backend as a retained safe transport edge because the six-step run is slightly positive, but do not spend the next optimization turn on more hot-weight all-reduce tuning. Existing W13 hot-split also regressed top-4 throughput, so the next MoE/EP work should preserve the standard-EP hot/cold/helper row partition and move toward first-class compact-row dispatcher layout, batched helper grouped VJP, or overlap of hot-weight movement with helper/owner math.
